speaker: CLEOMENES
text: The climate’s delicate, the air most sweet,

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: Fertile the isle, the temple much surpassing

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: The common praise it bears.

speaker: DION
text: I shall report,

speaker: DION
text: For most it caught me, the celestial habits—

speaker: DION
text: Methinks I so should term them—and the reverence

speaker: DION
text: Of the grave wearers. O, the sacrifice,

speaker: DION
text: How ceremonious, solemn, and unearthly

speaker: DION
text: It was i’ th’ off’ring!

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: But of all, the burst

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: And the ear-deaf’ning voice o’ th’ oracle,

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: Kin to Jove’s thunder, so surprised my sense

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: That I was nothing.

speaker: DION
text: If th’ event o’ th’ journey

speaker: DION
text: Prove as successful to the Queen—O, be ’t so!—

speaker: DION
text: As it hath been to us rare, pleasant, speedy,

speaker: DION
text: The time is worth the use on ’t.

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: Great Apollo

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: Turn all to th’ best! These proclamations,

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: So forcing faults upon Hermione,

speaker: CLEOMENES
text: I little like.

speaker: DION
text: The violent carriage of it

speaker: DION
text: Will clear or end the business when the oracle,

speaker: DION
text: Thus by Apollo’s great divine sealed up,

speaker: DION
text: Shall the contents discover. Something rare

speaker: DION
text: Even then will rush to knowledge. Go. Fresh horses;

speaker: DION
text: And gracious be the issue.
